title: Garlicky Creamy Poached Turkey Stir-fry
description: A paleo-friendly monsoon korean-inspired dish featuring poached turkey with a creamy garlic sauce.
instructions: Mince the garlic cloves and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add minced garlic and cook for about 2 minutes until softened, stirring occasionally.

Add turkey breast to the pot, turning to coat with garlic. Pour enough chicken broth into the pot to cover the meat completely. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and let simmer in the Dutch oven for approximately 1 hour or until the turkey is fully cooked (internal temperature of 165°F). Remove from heat.
In a medium bowl, mix heavy cream, sour cream, almond milk, and enough reserved cooking liquid from the pot to reach about 1 cup total. Stir in salt to taste.
Carefully transfer the poached turkey into a cutting board or plate. Let it cool for a few minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain (for paleo diet).
Place sliced turkey back into the Dutch oven with its cooking liquid. Slowly pour in prepared cream sauce, stirring constantly to avoid curdling.
Let simmer on low heat for about 10-15 minutes, allowing flavors to meld together and the sauce thickens slightly. Serve hot over cauliflower rice or a bed of spinach.
